seo.title: Invoicemonk vs Dext (2026): Honest Freelancer Comparison
seo.description: Dext captures receipts. Invoicemonk sends invoices. They solve opposite problems. Honest 2026 comparison for international freelancers — features, pricing, who needs which.

hero.title: Invoicemonk vs Dext (2026): An Honest Comparison for International Freelancers
hero.subtitle: Last updated: May 2026 · 12 minute read
hero.tagline: They aren't competitors. Dext captures the receipts coming in. Invoicemonk sends the invoices going out. Here's the honest comparison so you pick the right tool — or both.

quickAnswer.label: Quick answer
quickAnswer.body: Dext (formerly Receipt Bank) is an AI-powered bookkeeping automation platform used by over 700,000 businesses and 12,000 accounting firms worldwide. Its core product is capturing receipts and purchase invoices, extracting data with 99.9% accuracy using OCR and AI, and publishing that data automatically to accounting software like Xero, QuickBooks, and Sage. Dext does not create outbound invoices to send to clients, does not process client payments, and does not send payment reminders. If you are a freelancer looking for a tool to send invoices to your clients and get paid across borders, Dext is not that tool. Invoicemonk is purpose-built for cross-border freelance invoicing: compliant invoice generation, multi-currency support, automatic payment reminders, and payment tracking for freelancers in every country.

sections.heading: Why this comparison exists and why it matters
paragraphs: If you searched 'Invoicemonk vs Dext' or arrived on this page evaluating both tools, it is worth addressing something directly: Dext and Invoicemonk are not competing products. They solve fundamentally different problems in the financial workflow of a freelancer or small business.

Invoicemonk is an outbound invoicing platform. You use it to create invoices, send them to clients, accept payments, send automatic reminders when invoices are overdue, and track which of your invoices have been paid.

Dext is an inbound document capture platform. You use it to capture receipts, purchase invoices, and bank statements, extract the data from them automatically, and push that data into your accounting software so your bookkeeper or accountant does not have to enter it manually.
One tool faces outward toward your clients. The other faces inward toward your accountant and your books. They operate on opposite sides of your financial workflow.

This page exists because many freelancers evaluating their financial tools hear about both and wonder whether they overlap, whether one replaces the other, or whether they need one, the other, or both. The honest answer is that most international freelancers need something like Invoicemonk for their outbound invoicing and may benefit from something like Dext for their inbound expense management — but the two tools do not compete.

sections.heading: What Dext actually does
paragraphs: Dext was founded in 2010 under the name Receipt Bank and rebranded as Dext in 2021. According to Dext's own website, it is 'a bookkeeping automation platform that helps businesses save time and reduce manual work by automating the collection, processing, and storage of receipts, invoices and business documents.'

The workflow Dext is designed for runs as follows. You receive a purchase invoice from a supplier, or you pay for a business expense and receive a receipt. You photograph the receipt with Dext's mobile app, forward the invoice to your Dext email address, or allow Dext's Fetch feature to automatically retrieve invoices from supplier portals. Dext's AI extracts the key data from the document — vendor name, date, total amount, tax amount, currency, and invoice number. It categorises the expense based on your previous transactions and applies your automation rules. The processed data is published directly to your accounting software — Xero, QuickBooks, Sage, or one of more than 30 others — ready for reconciliation.

Dext does not generate invoices that you send to clients. It does not have a client database of the businesses that pay you. It does not send payment requests or payment links to your clients. It does not send payment reminders when a client has not paid. It does not track which of your client invoices are outstanding. These capabilities simply do not exist in the product because they are outside its designed purpose.

Dext's purpose is to eliminate the manual data entry and document management work that happens after you have already been paid or have already incurred an expense. It is a time-saving tool for the accounting and bookkeeping side of your business, not a tool for the invoicing and payment-collection side.

sections.heading: What Invoicemonk does
paragraphs: Invoicemonk is the outbound side of a freelancer's financial workflow. You use it to create invoices for your clients that comply with the legal requirements of both your country and the client's country. You send those invoices directly from the platform by email with a payment link embedded. You set automatic reminders to fire before, on, and after the due date without manual intervention. You track the status of every outstanding invoice across your client portfolio: sent, viewed, paid, or overdue. You accept payments from international clients in multiple currencies. You maintain a client database with billing history.
Where Dext captures the documents that come into your business, Invoicemonk generates the documents that go out from your business. They are the two ends of the same financial chain.

sections.heading: The structural difference: inbound vs outbound
paragraphs: Understanding this distinction is the most useful thing this comparison page can do for you. Here is the same freelancer's complete financial workflow showing where each tool operates:
1. You win a project with a client in the United States. You create a compliant invoice in Invoicemonk and send it with a payment link. You set a reminder. This is Invoicemonk's domain.
2. Your client pays. The payment is recorded in Invoicemonk. This is Invoicemonk's domain.

3. You pay for a software subscription to complete the project. You receive a receipt. You photograph it with Dext's mobile app. Dext extracts the data and publishes it to Xero as a business expense. This is Dext's domain.

4. At the end of the quarter, your accountant opens Xero. All your income from paid invoices and all your categorised expenses are already there, accurately recorded, ready for reconciliation and tax preparation. Both tools have contributed to this point.

Neither tool replaces the other. Neither tool does what the other does. A freelancer who uses Invoicemonk for outbound invoicing and Dext for inbound expense management has covered both sides of their financial workflow efficiently.

sections.heading: Who uses Dext
paragraphs: Dext's primary users, as documented by GetApp's analysis of verified reviews, are accounting professionals. Accountants represent 38% of Dext's reviewers, and accounting is the most frequently cited use case at 53% of reviews. The platform is fundamentally designed as a tool that accountants give to their clients so that clients can submit receipts easily, with the accountant receiving clean, extracted data ready for bookkeeping.

Dext's own marketing describes its primary audience as 'accountants, bookkeepers, and small businesses.' Its Practice plan, starting at $239.19 per month, is designed for accounting firms managing multiple client accounts. Its Business plan, starting at $31.50 per month, is for the businesses that those accountants serve.

For a freelancer who works with an accountant or bookkeeper, Dext can be a useful tool for submitting business expenses to your accountant cleanly, without a shoebox of receipts at year end. For a freelancer who manages their own books without professional accounting support, the value of Dext is more limited unless expense data entry is a significant bottleneck in their workflow.

rows.name: Create outbound invoices to send to clients
Send invoices to clients by email
Capture inbound receipts and purchase invoices
Publish data to Xero, QuickBooks, Sage
Expense management for your own costs
GDPR-compliant UK and EU cloud hosting

pricing.title: Pricing comparison
intro: Dext offers two categories of plans: Business plans for individual businesses and their teams, and Practice plans for accounting firms managing multiple client accounts. Invoicemonk has a single pricing ladder built around how many invoices you send and how many team members you have.

invoicemonk.title: Invoicemonk pricing (2026)

plans.name: Starter
plans.detail: 5 invoices/mo, 5 receipts/mo, up to 5 clients, 1 currency account, 1 user, watermark-free PDFs, accounting module, expense tracking, credit notes

plans.name: Pro
plans.detail: Unlimited invoices, receipts, clients, currency accounts. Up to 5 users. Custom branding, audit trail, data exports

plans.name: SME
plans.detail: Everything in Pro, plus online payments, advanced reports, premium templates, unlimited team

plans.name: Biz
plans.detail: E-invoicing & government submission, dedicated account manager, custom integrations, SLA

invoicemonk.note: Annual billing: pay for 10 months, get 12 (~17% off). Every plan includes compliant cross-border invoicing, multi-currency, accounting, and credit notes.

competitor.title: Dext pricing (2026)

plans.name: Business Essentials
plans.detail: 5 users, 250 documents/month. Receipt and invoice capture, expense management, bank feeds, accounting integration

plans.name: Business Advanced
plans.detail: Adds practice productivity features, enhanced automation rules, larger document allowances

plans.name: Practice Essentials
plans.detail: 10 client accounts. For accounting firms providing Dext to their clients. Annual: $207.99/mo (~13% off)

plans.name: Practice Advanced
plans.detail: Adds Workflows, practice-level Insights, Teams and Locations features. Pricing scales with client account volume

competitor.note: Plans available in USD, GBP, EUR, CAD, AUD, and ZAR. No free plan — 14-day trial only. Document allowance limits apply per month.

context: What the document allowance means in practice: Dext's pricing is partly based on document volume — the number of receipts, invoices, and statements processed each month. A freelancer with moderate expense activity (30–50 documents/month) stays comfortably within the 250-document allowance on Business Essentials. A freelancer with high expense activity may need to monitor usage against the limit.

Value calculation for individual freelancers: GetApp's analysis of 29 Dext pricing reviews notes that 'some users report that the pricing model is inconsistent and can be prohibitive for individuals or small businesses.' Reviewers indicate Dext delivers clear value for accounting firms and businesses with significant expense volumes, but 'some users find subscription pricing expensive, especially as the business grows.' For a solo freelancer whose primary task is sending invoices and collecting payments, $31.50/month for bookkeeping automation may not be justified unless expense management is a significant time cost or their accountant requires it.
pricing.summary: Invoicemonk is free to start and built for freelancers billing clients. Dext is paid-only and built for accountants processing client documents. Different jobs, different price models.

framework.title: Do you need one, the other, or both?
framework.intro: This is the practical question that matters most. Here is an honest framework for deciding.

items.title: Use Invoicemonk if your primary need is getting compliant invoices to international clients and getting paid reliably.
items.body: If you spend time worrying about whether your invoice will be accepted by your client's accounts payable team, whether your reminder will reach the right person, or whether you can clearly see which of your invoices are outstanding at any moment, Invoicemonk solves these problems directly.

items.title: Use Dext if your primary need is reducing the time you spend on expense management and bookkeeping data entry.
items.body: If you regularly incur business expenses, receive supplier invoices, and find yourself spending hours each month organising receipts and manually entering data into your accounting software — or if your accountant has asked you to submit expenses through a structured system — Dext solves these problems directly.

items.title: Use both if you have significant activity on both sides of your financial workflow.
items.body: An international freelancer generating $5,000–$20,000/month in client revenue, working with an accountant, incurring regular business expenses across multiple categories, and billing clients in multiple countries may genuinely benefit from both tools covering opposite ends of the same chain. Invoicemonk manages what goes out. Dext manages what comes in. Your accounting software reconciles both.

items.title: Use neither alone if you expect one to cover the other's job.
items.body: This is the scenario that causes the most frustration. A freelancer who signs up for Dext expecting to send client invoices will find the tool does not have that capability. A freelancer who uses only Invoicemonk for outbound invoicing and manually enters their own expenses into a spreadsheet is getting the outbound side right but not saving time on the inbound side.

strengths.title: Where Dext is genuinely excellent
strengths.intro: Dext is one of the best products in its category. Being honest about this matters — readers who feel misled stop trusting the source.

items.title: OCR accuracy is best in class
items.body: Dext claims 99.9% accuracy in extracting data from receipts and invoices, including handwritten and crumpled ones. Capterra reviewers who use Dext daily confirm the extraction is reliable and reduces manual correction to a minimum. For accountants managing high document volumes across many clients, this accuracy is genuinely transformative.

items.title: Accounting ecosystem integration is unmatched
items.body: Dext connects with over 30 accounting platforms, more than 11,500 banks and financial institutions, and e-commerce platforms including Shopify, Amazon, Stripe, Square, and PayPal. Its Xero and QuickBooks Online integrations are both rated 4.9/5 by verified GetApp users — the highest possible score.

items.title: The mobile app is practically useful
items.body: Photograph a receipt immediately after a purchase, have it automatically extracted and categorised, and have it published to your accounting software without further action. For business travellers and freelancers working across multiple locations, this real-time capture prevents the chaos of unprocessed receipts at tax time.

items.title: Seven-year document storage provides audit protection
items.body: Dext stores all captured documents with bank-level encryption for seven years. For freelancers in jurisdictions that require records to be kept for specific periods for tax purposes, this creates a compliant digital archive automatically as a byproduct of normal use.

items.title: The accountant workflow is purpose-built
items.body: If you work with an accountant or bookkeeper, having them connected to your Dext account means they receive clean, extracted, categorised data instead of raw documents and spreadsheets. Multiple Capterra reviewers note their accountants introduced them to Dext specifically because it reduces the accountant's preparation time — which often translates to lower accounting fees for the client.

scenarios.title: The freelancer scenario: two different problems

items.title: Scenario A — Getting paid by an international client
items.body:
You are a freelance developer based in Lagos. You have completed a project for a startup in Amsterdam worth $4,500. You need to send a compliant invoice, follow up if it is not paid on time, and track when payment arrives.

Dext cannot help with this scenario. It is not an outbound invoicing tool and has no capability for creating or sending client invoices.

Invoicemonk handles this end to end. You create a compliant invoice that meets the requirements for a Nigeria-to-Netherlands cross-border transaction. You send it from the platform with an embedded payment link. A reminder fires automatically three days before the due date and again seven days after if the invoice remains unpaid. Your dashboard shows the invoice as paid when the funds arrive.

items.title: Scenario B — Submitting business expenses to your accountant
items.body:
Same freelance developer. You subscribe to three software tools, pay for a co-working space membership, and purchase equipment for a project. You need to submit these expenses to your accountant accurately, without spending hours gathering receipts and entering amounts into a spreadsheet.

Invoicemonk does not handle this scenario. It manages your outbound invoices, not your inbound expenses.

Dext handles this end to end. You photograph the receipt for the equipment purchase immediately after buying it. You forward the software subscription invoices to your Dext email address. You allow Dext to auto-fetch the co-working invoice from the provider's portal. Dext extracts all the data, categorises each expense, and publishes it to your accounting software ready for your accountant to reconcile, without you manually entering a single figure.

scenarios.footer: Both scenarios are real parts of a freelancer's financial life. They require different tools.

userReviews.title: What real users say about Dext
paragraphs: Dext's user review record is strong in its intended context. Capterra reviewers from accounting practices describe it as a tool they 'couldn't be without' and a 'go-to for the foundation of our app stacks.' The most consistent praise is for its accuracy, its Xero integration, and the time it saves on data entry. Multiple reviewers describe the before-and-after transformation of their bookkeeping workflow as significant.

The criticisms that appear in verified reviews are relevant to the freelancer audience. GetApp's analysis of 29 pricing-focused reviews finds that 41% are positive about value, but a meaningful proportion of reviewers find the pricing 'inconsistent and prohibitive for individuals or small businesses.' Some users report that 'cancelling subscriptions is difficult and customer service is lacking,' a concern that appears across multiple independent sources.

One Capterra reviewer noted that the email-in feature occasionally does not work and the system sometimes cannot read supplier names from a receipt, requiring manual correction. This is a minor limitation given the platform's overall accuracy, but it illustrates that the 99.9% accuracy figure is not absolute.

For freelancers considering Dext, the clearest signal from user reviews is that its value scales with volume and professional accounting support. A freelancer with an accountant who uses Xero or QuickBooks and who processes dozens of receipts and supplier invoices per month will experience a significant return on the $31.50/month Business plan. A freelancer doing their own bookkeeping with limited business expenses may not.

bottomLine.title: The bottom line
paragraphs: Dext is an excellent product that does exactly what it is designed to do. Its AI-powered receipt capture, 99.9% OCR accuracy, and seamless integration with Xero and QuickBooks make it genuinely valuable for the accountant-client workflow it is built to serve. Over 700,000 businesses and 12,000 accounting firms use it, and the strong review record across Capterra, G2, and GetApp reflects a product that consistently delivers on its core promise.

The important truth for international freelancers evaluating both tools is that Dext solves a different problem from Invoicemonk. If your primary need is sending compliant invoices to international clients, getting paid across borders, and tracking which of your invoices are outstanding, Dext cannot help you with that. You need an outbound invoicing platform — and that is what Invoicemonk is built to be.

If your primary need is reducing bookkeeping admin, submitting expenses to an accountant automatically, and eliminating the data entry that comes with managing receipts and supplier invoices, Dext is one of the strongest tools in that category.

Many international freelancers doing meaningful cross-border work need both. Invoicemonk covers the client-facing side of your financial workflow. Dext covers the accountant-facing side. Together, they close the loop.

items.question: Can Dext send invoices to my clients?
items.answer: No. Dext captures and processes inbound financial documents (receipts, purchase invoices, bank statements) and publishes the extracted data to accounting software. It does not create or send outbound invoices to clients. For outbound invoicing, you need a dedicated invoicing tool such as Invoicemonk.

items.question: Does Dext process client payments?
items.answer: No. Dext does not process payments of any kind. It is a document capture and data extraction platform, not a payment processor or invoicing platform.

items.question: Can Dext send automatic payment reminders?
items.answer: No. Payment reminders are a feature of outbound invoicing tools. Dext has no reminder functionality because it is not designed for the client billing workflow.

items.question: What is Dext best used for by freelancers?
items.answer: Dext is most valuable to freelancers who work with an accountant or bookkeeper and want to submit business expenses cleanly and automatically, or who spend significant time each month manually entering receipt and invoice data into accounting software. It eliminates that data entry work through AI-powered OCR extraction and direct integration with accounting software.

items.question: Does Dext work for freelancers outside the UK and US?
items.answer: Yes. According to G2's pricing documentation, Dext's plans are available in USD, GBP, EUR, CAD, AUD, and ZAR. The platform connects with over 11,500 banks and financial institutions worldwide, making it practically usable in most countries.

items.question: What was Dext previously called?
items.answer: Dext was previously called Receipt Bank. The company rebranded from Receipt Bank to Dext in 2021. Dext Prepare is the new name for the core Receipt Bank product.

items.question: Does Invoicemonk integrate with Dext?
items.answer: Invoicemonk and Dext operate on different sides of the financial workflow and do not currently integrate directly. Invoicemonk manages your outbound invoicing and payment collection. Dext manages your inbound document capture and expense processing. Both can feed into your accounting software, where your records are consolidated.

items.question: Can I use both Invoicemonk and Dext together?
items.answer: Yes — and for many international freelancers with professional accounting support and regular business expenses, using both makes sense. Invoicemonk handles outbound invoices and payments from clients. Dext handles inbound receipts and expenses going to your accountant. They are not duplicates.
